chapter_context.summary: The three annual feasts: Passover, Weeks (Pentecost), and Booths (Tabernacles).

outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Passover (16:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Deuteronomy.
This section focuses on The Feast of Weeks (16:9-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Deuteronomy.
This section focuses on The Feast of Booths (16:13-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Deuteronomy.
This section focuses on Judges and Justice (16:18-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Deuteronomy.

key_themes.title: Covenant Loyalty
key_themes.description: The requirement to love and obey God from the heart.

key_themes.title: Remembrance
key_themes.description: The importance of recalling God's acts to maintain faith.

devotional.question: How can we 'circumcise our hearts' today as Moses commanded the Israelites?
What blessings have you seen in your life as a result of choosing obedience?

cultural_historical.title: The Second Law
cultural_historical.text: Deuteronomy is not a new law but a 're-iteration' (deutero-nomos) for the generation about to enter the land.
